data.title: Naver's Financial to buy South Korean crypto exchange operator in $10 billion deal
data.description:
• Naver Financial, the payment arm of South Korean internet giant Naver (OTCPK:NHNCF) to acquire Dunamu, the operator of Upbit, a cryptocurrency exchange in South Korea.
  • The acquisition will take place in an all-stock deal valued at ~$10.27 billion (15.13 trillion won).
  •  The deal involves Naver Financial issuing 2.54 shares for every one share held in Dunamu.
  • The transaction positions Naver (OTCPK:NHNCF) to capitalize on a rapidly growing cryptocurrency market in South Korea.

data.title: Alibaba starts selling Quark AI glasses in China, enters wearable race
data.description:
China's e-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA) has started selling its Quark AI smart glasses, powered by the Qwen and Quark's multimodal AI model, marking its entry into the consumer wearable tech segment.

The Quark S1 is designed with translucent displays that place digital layers over real-world views and includes built-in translucent micro-OLED displays for contextual information. It is equipped with cameras, bone conduction, microphones, and swappable batteries that are built to last up to 24 hours. 

Alongside the S1, which starts at 3,799 yuan ($537), Alibaba (BABA) offers a 1,899-yuan Quark G1 model. Both are available now via Alibaba's Tmall, JD.com, Douyin, and over 600 stores in 82 Chinese cities, with international versions planned for 2026 on platforms like AliExpress, according to a Bloomberg report.

The release extends Alibaba's (BABA) AI push following the Qwen app's 10 million users and integration into its Quark browser. This positions the company against Meta (META) in the wearables market.

data.title: China market regulator issues competition compliance guidance for smartphone sector: report
data.description:
China's market regulator has issued an anti-unfair competition compliance guidance for smartphone manufacturers and mobile application platform companies in Shenzhen, Reuters reported, citing Chinese state media.

No companies were named in the report.

China's smartphone market is dominated by companies such as vivo, Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) (OTCPK:XIACY), Huawei Technologies, Apple (AAPL), Oppo, and Honor.

Earlier this month it was reported that Apple's (AAPL) iPhone sales in China have recently surged, driving the company to capture 25% of the Chinese smartphone market. Separately, according to Counterpoint Research, iPhone sales in China soared 22% compared to a year ago levels in the first month after the iPhone 17 series launched, even as the broader market softened.

data.title: Europe markets in green on hopes of December Fed rate cute
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.05% to 9,697. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.35% to 23,808, making the fourth session of gains. Germany’s GfK Consumer Climate Indicator rose to -23.2 heading into December.

France (CAC:IND) +0.10% to 8,104. 

In other parts of Europe, Sweden’s Economic Tendency Indicator rose to 101.7 in November. Sweden’s business confidence barometer rose to 105.8 in November.

The industrial confidence indicator in Slovakia increased in November.

Norway’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate fell to 4.5% in October.

Finland’s consumer confidence rose to -6.5 in November. Finland's manufacturing confidence declined to -6 in November.

Business confidence in the Netherlands fell to -1.7 in November.

Retail sales in Denmark increased by 4.9% Y/Y in October.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) up 0.12% to 574.9, as market sentiment was supported by rising expectations of a December Fed rate cut and hopes of an end to the war in Ukraine. The euro traded close to the $1.16 mark, its strongest level since mid-November, as investors prepared for a data-heavy end to the month.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was down 1 basis point to 3.99%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 2.68%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.45%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Linkage Global announces $1M PIPE financing
data.description:
• Linkage Global (LGCB) announced the pricing of a private investment in public equity financing.
  • On November 24, the company entered into a securities purchase agreement pursuant to which it will issue and sell an aggregate of 689,655 Class A Ordinary Shares to an investor for $1.45 per share. 
  • The offering will result in total gross proceeds of approximately $1,000,000 before deducting fees and offering expenses payable by the company.
  • Subject to customary closing conditions, the PIPE is expected to close on or before December 3, 2025.

data.title: Taiwan unveils $40B defense budget for U.S. weapons, T-Dome amid China threat
data.description:
Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te has unveiled a special $40B defense budget over the next eight years, following U.S. pressure on the island to boost military spending. 

"This landmark package will not only fund significant new arms acquisitions from the U.S., but also vastly enhance Taiwan's asymmetrical capabilities," Lai announced in an op-ed in the Washington Post.

He said Taiwan will ramp up development of the 'T-Dome' - a multilayered defense system to protect the island from PLA missiles, rockets, drones, and combat aircraft. 

Lai pointed to China's "unprecedented military buildup" and "intensifying provocations" in the Taiwan Strait, East and South China Seas and across the Indo-Pacific. "Beijing's willingness to alter the status quo by force has become increasingly evident."

In response to growing pressure from Beijing, Taiwan's defense spending is expected to rise to 3.3% of GDP by next year. Lai said he's committed to lifting this baseline to 5% by 2030.

President Donald Trump previously called on Taiwan to spend up to 10% of its GDP on defense.

The U.S. State Department welcomed Taiwan's budget announcement, which "demonstrates resolve to strengthen Taiwan's self-defense capabilities," and the U.S. supports the island's "acquisition of critical defense capabilities, commensurate with the threat it faces."

"China opposes official interactions and military ties between the U.S. and China's Taiwan region," said Mao Ning, Chinese foreign ministry's spokesperson. She added that the Taiwanese government "will not succeed in resisting reunification and seeking 'independence' through military buildup."

data.title: Asahi delays financial report after cyberattack, says cannot avoid short-term impact on Japan operations
data.description:
Japanese brewer Asahi (OTCPK:ASBRF) on Thursday said that it has not received any specific demands from the hackers behind a “sophisticated and cunning” cyberattack that is now nearing its third month.

The company added that the attack will delay its financial results by more than 50 days after the close of the fiscal year.

"The fiscal 2025 third-quarter results, full-year results, and information relating to the impact of system disruption on overall corporate performance will be disclosed as soon as possible once the systems have been restored and the relevant data confirmed," the company said in a statement.

Core operating profit from the Japan & East Asia segment is being negatively impacted by the system disruptions to a certain degree, while profit performance in the Europe and Asia Pacific segments remains largely in line with the plan, Asahi added.

"We cannot avoid this short-term impact on our Japan operations."

"We have not been in touch with the attacker," CEO Atsushi Katsuki said at a news conference, as the company again delayed the release of its financial results.

"Even if we had a ransom demand, we would not have paid it," he said.

data.title: Puma gains on report that China's Anta Sports is considering a bid
data.description:
Chinese sports apparel company Anta Sports Products Ltd. is reportedly among firms exploring a potential takeover of German sportswear maker Puma SE (OTCPK:PMMAF).

Hong Kong-listed Anta has been working with an adviser to evaluate a bid for Puma (OTCPK:PMMAF), Bloomberg News reported. The company may team up with a private equity firm if it decides to move forward with an offer.

Frankfurt stock exchange-listed shares in Puma were up 7% in early trading.

Other potential bidders could include Chinese sportswear group Li Ning (OTCPK:LNNGF), which has been discussing financing options with banks as it takes an early look at Puma, according to the report. Puma may also attract interest from a Japanese sportswear company, the report said.

Puma unveiled a turnaround plan and announced job cuts in October, stating that it expects both industry-wide and company-specific challenges to significantly impact performance through the remainder of 2025 amid ongoing volatile geopolitical and macroeconomic conditions.

data.title: Netflix recovers after hour-long outage during ‘Stranger Things’ final season release
data.description:
• Netflix (NFLX) experienced about an hour of downtime for many U.S. users on Wednesday evening, coinciding with the debut of the fifth and final season of its popular series Stranger Things.
  • "Some members briefly experienced an issue streaming on TV devices, but service recovered for all accounts within five minutes," Netflix told Reuters.
  • The streaming platform has ranked "Stranger Things" as its third-most popular English-language series globally. The highly anticipated season was set to be released at 8 p.m. ET on Wednesday.

data.title: Chinese tech firms said to train AI models abroad to tap Nvidia chips
data.description:
Chinese tech companies are training their artificial intelligence models overseas to access Nvidia's (NVDA) chips, the Financial Times reported, citing two people with direct knowledge of the matter. 

Companies such as Alibaba (BABA) and ByteDance (BDNCE) are said to be training their latest AI models in Southeast Asian data centers. The companies rely on lease agreements for data centers owned and operated by non-Chinese entities.

The shift in AI training is aimed at bypassing U.S. measures to restrict sales of Nvidia's (NVDA) chips, like the H20, in China.

Notably, DeepSeek's (DEEPSEEK) model is being trained in China as the AI startup reportedly secured a large stock of Nvidia (NVDA) chips before the U.S. imposed export controls.

DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) is also working with local chipmakers like Huawei to develop the next generation of Chinese AI chips.

The report comes as China has been discouraging local tech companies from buying advanced Nvidia (NVDA) chips, and recently banned the use of foreign AI chips in state-funded data centers.

Meanwhile, President Donald Trump is considering whether to let Nvidia sell H200 chips to China.
